metadata.title: Reports and Indices Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: According to recent study, which city has been ranked the best city for women in India in 2025?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru has been ranked the best city for women in India in 2025, according to a study by Chennai-based workplace inclusion firm Avtar. The study assessed 125 cities on how effectively they support women through a combination of social infrastructure and industrial inclusion. Bengaluru topped the list with a City Inclusion Score (CIS) of 53.29, followed by Chennai (49.86), Pune (46.27), Hyderabad (46.04), and Mumbai (44.49). The index is based on two pillars: Social Inclusion Score (SIS), which measures safety, health, education, mobility, and liveability, and Industrial Inclusion Score (IIS), which tracks formal job opportunities, corporate inclusion practices, skilling, and women’s workforce participation.

questions.question: Which organization released the World Economic Situation and Prospects 2026 report?

options.label: A
options.text: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)

options.label: C
options.text: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UNDESA)

answer.label: C
answer.text: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UNDESA)

questions.explanation: The United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UNDESA) released the World Economic Situation and Prospects 2026 report. India’s GDP growth for 2025-26 is projected at 7.2%, slightly below the 7.4% forecast by the Indian government. India is now the fourth largest economy in the world. Growth is expected to be supported by resilient consumption, strong public investment, recent tax reforms, and monetary easing, which largely offset the impact of higher U.S. tariffs. On a fiscal year basis, India’s GDP is projected to grow 6.6% in 2026-27 and 6.8% in 2027-28.

questions.question: Which state secured the first rank in the Export Preparedness Index (EPI) 2024 under the Large States category?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog released the Export Preparedness Index (EPI) 2024, assessing export readiness of India’s States and Union Territories (UTs) to support the goal of USD 1 trillion merchandise exports by 2030 and Viksit Bharat @2047. EPI 2024 is structured around four pillars—Export Infrastructure, Business Ecosystem, Policy & Governance, and Export Performance. It is further divided into 13 sub-pillars and 70 indicators for granular assessment. Top-performing Large States are Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, and Andhra Pradesh, while top-performing Small/North Eastern States & UTs are Uttarakhand, Jammu and Kashmir, Nagaland, Dadra & Nagar Haveli & Daman and Diu, and Goa.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in Asia Manufacturing Index (AMI) 2026?
questions.explanation: India ranked sixth among 11 Asian countries in the Asia Manufacturing Index (AMI) 2026, showing the need to strengthen manufacturing competitiveness. The Asia Manufacturing Index (AMI) measures competitiveness across eight key pillars and 43 sub-parameters. The index is in its third year and is released by Dezan Shira and Associates. Malaysia rose to second place in 2026, replacing Vietnam. Vietnam slipped to third position. These changes indicate intensifying competition within Asia’s manufacturing sector.

questions.question: Which city has been ranked the world’s safest city for the tenth consecutive year, according to 2026 Numbeo Global Safety Index?
questions.explanation: Abu Dhabi ranked the world’s safest city for the tenth consecutive year since 2017. The ranking was released by Numbeo, a global cost of living and quality of life database. The achievement highlights Abu Dhabi’s strong security, stability, and high quality of life. It strengthens the emirate’s image as a top destination for living, working, and investment.

questions.question: Which organisation released the report titled “Roadmap for Green Transition of MSMEs”?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

options.label: D
options.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

questions.explanation: NITI Aayog released the report “Roadmap for Green Transition of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)” aligned with Viksit Bharat 2047 and net-zero by 2070. It is a 10-year action plan for 69 million MSMEs to shift towards low-carbon growth. The roadmap focuses on energy efficiency, green electricity, and alternative fuels. It proposes a National Project Management Agency (NPMA) for implementation and finance support. MSMEs contribute about 30% to Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and 45.7% to exports, employing over 250 million people. They emitted around 135 million tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) in 2022.

questions.question: Which state bagged the 1st position in the PESA (Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas) State Rankings among the Fifth Schedule Areas?
questions.explanation: PESA (Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas) State Rankings for 2024–25 were released. Maharashtra secured 1st position, Madhya Pradesh 2nd, and Himachal Pradesh 3rd among the 10 States with Fifth Schedule Areas. The rankings assess implementation of the PESA Act, which strengthens Gram Sabhas and Panchayats in Scheduled Areas. The rankings aim to promote Gram Sabha-centric self-governance, evidence-based policy interventions, and identify areas needing focused support. The initiative reflects a collective commitment of the Centre and States for outcome-oriented PESA implementation.

questions.question: Which state achieved top ranks in the Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) 2.0?
questions.explanation: Tripura has achieved top ranks in the Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) 2.0, highlighting excellence in its Panchayati Raj system. Three Tripura Panchayats among the top four reflect strong grassroots governance and efficiency in the state. The recognition sets a benchmark for rural development, inclusive governance, and public service delivery in Tripura. It reinforces Tripura’s reputation as a model state in decentralized governance and village-level administration.

questions.question: The Global Appeal 2026 report has been released by which organization?

options.label: A
options.text: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR)

questions.explanation: The Global Appeal 2026 report was recently released by the International Organization for Migration (IOM). International Organization for Migration (IOM) requested USD 4.7 billion to support 41 million people worldwide in 2026. The report aligns with IOM’s Strategic Plan 2024–2028 under a humanitarian reset. It highlights record internal displacement of 83.4 million people globally. Climate-related displacement rose sharply to 9.8 million in 2024. 2024 was the deadliest year for migrants with over 9,197 deaths. A major USD 3.4 billion funding gap remains, threatening migrant support efforts.

questions.question: The policy report titled “Revitalizing Apprenticeship Ecosystem” was released by which institution?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: NITI Aayog launched a report titled “Revitalizing Apprenticeship Ecosystem: Insights, Challenges, Recommendations and Best Practices.” The report analyses India’s apprenticeship system and suggests reforms to strengthen skilling and employment. It gives 20 action-oriented recommendations with clear responsibilities and performance metrics. Recommendations are grouped under five pillars including policy reforms, regulatory strengthening, state-level action, industry engagement and apprentice support. It aligns apprenticeship growth with Viksit Bharat 2047 and global best practices to build a skilled and competitive workforce.
